markdownRemark.html:
Problem

Currently pypiserver does not support S3 as storage ( issue link )

Solution

Create a file as s3_packages_sync.sh which syncs /root/pypi/packages/ path in container to s3 path. For example -

#! /bin/sh
aws s3 sync /root/pypi/packages/ s3://$S3_PATH --region ap-south-1 --exclude '*' --include '*.whl' --include '*.tar.gz' | sed -e "s/^/$(date -R) /" \
>> /root/pypi/logs/s3_sync.log

Create a Dockerfile as follows

FROM python:3.8-alpine3.12

RUN apk update && apk add bash

RUN pip install awscli passlib==1.7.2 pypiserver==1.3.2

RUN mkdir -p /root/pypi
COPY ./htpasswd.txt ./pypi_homepage.html /root/pypi/

RUN mkdir -p /root/pypi/packages/
COPY s3_packages_sync.sh /etc/periodic/hourly/s3_sync_packages
RUN chmod u+x /etc/periodic/hourly/s3_sync_packages

Now run pypiserver with docker run command as follows

bash -c "crond && pypi-server -p 8898 -P /root/pypi/htpasswd.txt -a list,update,download --disable-fallback --welcome /root/pypi/pypi_homepage.html /root/pypi/packages"

Note:

  • Inject the AWS credentials if required
  • Since cron runs hourly, so publishing to S3 will at most take an hour.

frontmatter.title: Private 'pypiserver' with AWS S3 as backend storage
frontmatter.description: Hack to host private pypi python package repository with S3 as storage backend
